title: Australia and Germany strike cruise missile deal | 7NEWS
description: A German warhead manufacturer TDW and Australia's Department of Defence have signed a letter of intent to produce high-tech cruise missiles in Australia. The agreement was witnessed by both nations' defence ministers at Amberley Air Base in Queensland, where German military officials also inspected Australia's new autonomous Ghost Bat fighter jets. The Ghost Bat aircraft are scheduled to join the RAAF's arsenal in the coming years. title: Five charged over Sydney mother's brutal murder | 7NEWS
description: Five people have been charged over the abduction and murder of 45-year-old mother Kim Tran in Sydney nearly a year ago. Police allege a kill crew was hired to murder her husband, but when he failed to appear, they kidnapped Kim Tran instead, shooting her and burning her body in a car. An 8-year-old boy was assaulted with a baseball bat during the attack, and four other men already face charges in connection with the case. title: Construction site linked to fatal hospital mould outbreak | 7NEWS
description: A construction site at Royal Prince Alfred Hospital in Sydney likely caused a fatal aspergillus mould outbreak that killed three patients and left another in intensive care four months later. A New South Wales Health committee investigation found that testing and cleaning audits at the hospital failed to meet delivery standards. The aspergillus spores were probably aerosolised from construction work and travelled to a balcony area where patients were exposed. title: HCF tops list for private health complaints | 7NEWS
description: Australians lodged more than 3,400 complaints about private health insurers, with HCF receiving the highest number of complaints from dissatisfied customers. The majority of grievances involved claims being underpaid or denied entirely, while other customers faced difficulties cancelling their cover or obtaining refunds after switching providers. The watchdog has revealed the insurance companies with the highest complaint rates. title: Maduro faces narco-terrorism charges in New York court | 7NEWS
description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife appeared in a New York court facing narco-terrorism charges. The judge refused to dismiss the case against the dictator, while protesters gathered outside the courthouse. U.S. President Donald Trump suggested additional charges may be filed, including claims about Venezuelan prisoners being released into the United States, though no evidence supports this assertion. title: Real-life D'Artagnan skeleton found beneath Dutch church | 7NEWS
description: A skeleton discovered beneath a Dutch church may be the remains of Charles de Batz-Castelmore, the real-life 17th-century French musketeer who inspired the fictional character D'Artagnan in The Three Musketeers. The soldier was killed in battle in 1673, and scientists are planning DNA tests to confirm the identity of the remains found in the Netherlands. title: IOC bans transgender athletes from women's events | 7NEWS
description: The International Olympic Committee has banned transgender athletes from competing in women's events following a two-year review that identified clear advantages in strength, power and endurance. The decision takes immediate effect and has been welcomed by many top female athletes. The ruling establishes guidelines for Brisbane 2032, seeking to prevent controversies similar to those experienced in Paris. title: Sarah Ferguson stripped of York honour over Epstein ties | 7NEWS
description: Sarah Ferguson has been unanimously stripped of her Freedom of the City of York title, an honour bestowed upon her in 1987. York City Council voted to remove the title due to ongoing reputational damage caused by her association with the Jeffrey Epstein scandal through her former husband, Prince Andrew. Prince Andrew lost the same honour in 2022 after settling with his late accuser, Virginia Giuffre. title: Savannah Guthrie's guilt over mother's abduction | 7NEWS
description: US television host Savannah Guthrie has revealed the agonising guilt she feels over her mother Nancy's abduction, fearing the 84-year-old was targeted because of her daughter's fame. Fifty-four days after her mother's disappearance, Guthrie believes it was a kidnap for ransom, with two Bitcoin demand notes sent to American news organisations. Despite a $1 million reward, police remain no closer to finding Nancy, and Guthrie is speaking out to prevent the trail from going cold. title: ASX falls but heads for first positive week | 7NEWS
description: The ASX 200 declined approximately two-thirds of 1% following a weak lead from Wall Street, where the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell almost 2.5% amid mixed headlines on US-Iran peace talks. Despite the daily losses, the Australian index remains on track for its first positive week since conflict began nearly a month ago, with the energy sector leading gains while mining, technology and communications sectors face pressure. The Australian dollar has suffered its worst week since April of last year, falling roughly 2% to sit around 68.8 US cents. title: The hidden dangers of online dating apps | 7NEWS
description: More than 5 million Australians have used dating apps to find love, with 54% reporting long-term relationships from these platforms. However, Australians lost approximately $28 million to dating scams last year, with fraudsters using tactics like love bombing and fabricated emergencies to extract money from vulnerable users. Safety measures include keeping conversations on dating apps, reverse image searching profile photos, limiting personal information sharing, and never sending money or identity documents to online matches. title: Active middle-aged women halve early death risk | 7NEWS
description: Australian researchers tracked more than 11,000 women over 23 years and discovered that middle-aged women who engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate to vigorous physical activity per week can halve their risk of early death. The long-term study found these active women were significantly less likely to pass away before reaching their mid-70s compared to those who remained inactive. title: Parents pull kids from sport over concussion fears | 7NEWS
description: More than one in four children across Australia have stopped playing contact sports due to parental concerns about head injuries and concussion. The trend has extended beyond contact sports, with some parents now restricting their children's participation in non-contact activities and outdoor play. Experts warn this protective approach may be excessive and could negatively affect children's physical and mental wellbeing. title: Flooding cancels iconic Big Red Bash festival | 7NEWS
description: The Birdsville Big Red Bash, an outback music festival on the edge of the Simpson Desert, has been cancelled due to flooding at the site. Organisers were unable to find an alternative location for the iconic event. The majority of this year's line-up have committed to perform at the 2027 festival, with ticket holders offered refunds, transfers to next year, or transfers to this year's Mundi Mundi Bash in Broken Hill. title: Deadly hospital mould at RPA Hospital linked to spores from construction site
description: A balcony exposed to spores from a construction site was the most likely source of a fatal mould outbreak at one of Sydney's biggest hospitals, according to a new report. #mould #health #hospital #sydney #nsw
